name: Mary Rogers's Sourdough Biscuits
recipeInstructions:
1. At bedtime, make a batter with the 1/2 cup of starter, 1 cup of milk, and 1 cup of flour. Allow to set overnight if biscuits are desired for breakfast. If they are meant for lunch, the batter can be mixed in the morning and left in a warm place to rise.
2. Regardless of temperature, it is best to let the dough ferment overnight; it will become very light and bubbly.
3. Sift together the remaining 1 1/2 cups of flour and all dry ingredients except baking soda.
4. Work in the lard or shortening with fingers or a fork.
5. Add baking soda dissolved in warm water to the batter, then add the flour mixture. Mix until it forms a soft dough.
6. Knead lightly a few times to help it form shape.
7. Roll out to about 1/2 inch thickness (or slightly thicker) and cut with a biscuit cutter. Place closely together in a 9 x 13-inch pan, turning to grease the tops. Cover and let rise in a warm place for about 45 minutes.
8. Bake at 375°F for 30-35 minutes.
9. Leftovers are good when split and toasted in a sandwich toaster.
